Cheltenham Road, Gloucester, GL2 9QH, England Telephone: +44 (0) 1452 712424 www.safran-landing-systems.com

# SERVICE BULLETIN

LANDING GEAR - MAIN LANDING GEAR COMPLETE - INTRODUCTION OF A NEW CAP

- 1. Planning Information


- A. Effectivity

Unit affected: Main Landing Gear Complete which includes: Shock Strut Assembly Complete which includes: Shock Strut Dressings

Part/Type Nos.: 10-355503-001 (Main Landing Gear Complete LH)

- 10-355603-001 (Main Landing Gear Complete RH) or

- 10-355504-000 (Main Landing Gear Complete LH)

10-355604-000 (Main Landing Gear Complete RH) or

- 10-355505-000 (Main Landing Gear Complete LH)


- 10-355605-000 (Main Landing Gear Complete RH) which includes: 10-355703-001 (Shock Strut Assembly Complete LH) 10-355803-001 (Shock Strut Assembly Complete RH) which includes: 10-357303 Series (Shock Strut Dressings LH) 10-357403 Series (Shock Strut Dressings RH)


- B. Concurrent Requirements

(1) None.

- C. Reason Aircraft affected: Airbus A350-900

(1) A new cap is introduced in the bogie disconnect location of the 1M Strut harness to avoid the risk of chafing.

- D. Description


- (1) Safran Landing Systems tells operators that a new cap is introduced to prevent contact between the 1M strut harness and a bolt in the location of the sliding piston fork.
- (2) Safran Landing Systems recommend that you do this Service Bulletin on main landing gears on which:


- the strike number 10 has not been cancelled on the 10-357303 LH Series and 10-357403 RH Series shock strut dressings nameplate and looks like this .

EDES2-0037-0337 Oct 22/2019

Service Bulletin No. 10-355-32-29 Page 1 of 9

- E. Compliance

(1) Recommended.

- F. Approval

(1) The technical content of this document is approved under the authority of DOA ref. EASA.21J.031.

- G. Manpower

(1) Safran Landing Systems make an estimate that it will take approximately 1 man-hour to do this Service Bulletin for each main landing gear leg. The elapsed time is 1 hour. NOTE: It is assumed that the equipment has been placed in a maintenance

status. The man-hour estimates do not include preparation, close out, administrative functions or rework procedure if applicable.

- H. Weight and Balance

(1) There will be an increase in weight of 0,030 kg (0.0661 lb) for each main landing gear. A total increase in weight for the aircraft of 0,060 kg (0.1322 lb).

- I. Electrical Load Data

(1) Not changed.

- J. Software Accomplishment Summary

(1) Not applicable.

- K. References

- (1) Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Component Maintenance Manual 32-13-06.
- (2) Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Modification Form No. EDES2-0037-0337, issue 1.
- (3) This Service Bulletin refers to Process Specifications (M-DLPS, M-DLNDT and PCS): these are available in the Safran Landing Systems Technical Publications on-line service.
- (4) This Service Bulletin is subject to aircraft modification No. 112981 L04001 (classified minor) and is covered by aircraft Service Bulletin A350-32-P031. Under no circumstances should the modified equipment, resulting from the application of this SB, be installed on the aircraft type unless its aircraft SB is approved.


- L. Publications Affected

- (1) Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Component Maintenance Manual 32-13-02.
- (2) Safran Landing Systems UK Ltd Component Maintenance Manual 32-13-06.


- M. Interchangeability


- (1) Refer to aircraft Service Bulletin A350-32-P031.
- (2) For the interchangeability and intermixability of the unit on the Safran Landing Systems landing gear and the constituent parts of that unit refer to this Service Bulletin and applicable Safran Landing Systems CMM/IPLs.


- 2. Material Information


- A. Material - Price and Availability

(1) The prices shown in this material section are valid for the calendar year, ending December 31st 2019. Refer to the Safran Landing Systems Spare Parts Price Catalog for subsequent year prices or contact your regional Safran Landing Systems Customer Support Centers (CSC) for the latest price and availability information.

- B. Industry Support information

(1) Not applicable.

- C. List of components


The I/D codes below give information which help you to understand the instruction/disposition.

I/D Code Instruction/information

- A New part
- B Part that can be obtained from the old one
- C Expendable part
- D Old part to be discarded
- E Part of new design valid for new manufacture
- F Part number change only


Interchangeability codes (IC) given below show basic information to help you understand the interchangeability of parts. For full explanation of interchangeability codes, please refer to the latest edition of ATA specification 2000.

IC Instruction/information

- 01 One-way interchangeable
- 02 Two-way interchangeable
- 03 Not interchangeable
- 04 Interchangeable as a set
- 05 Qualified Interchangeability


The table that follows tells operators of the items that are necessary to do this Service Bulletin. It does not include tools or materials that are referred to in the Component Maintenance Manuals. Prices shown in the table that follows are for a quantity 1 of each part.

|New Part No.|Qty. Per Unit|Keyword|Old Part No.|Instructions/ Disposition|IC|New Part Price ($US)|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|55-3573317-00|1|Upper Cap|-|(A)|-|110.37|
|55-3573319-00|1|Lower Cap|-|(A)|-|120.56|
|MS24665-155|2|Cotter Pin|-|(A)|-|3.29|

- D. Subcomponents to be re-identified

(1) None.

- E. Tooling

(1) None.

- F. Special Tools


(1) None.

- 3. Accomplishment Instructions


The figure/item numbers in parentheses in the text agree with those that are in the illustrated parts list. Only the primary figure/item numbers are used. The Process Specifications (M-DLPS, M-DLNDT and PCS) are available in the Safran Landing Systems Technical Publications on-line service.

- A. General

(1) Not applicable.

- B. Preparation

(1) Refer to Airbus Service Bulletin A350-32-P031.

- C. Procedure


(1) Procedure (refer to CMM 32-13-06 and Figures 1 to 3 of this Service Bulletin).

- (a) Remove the cotter pins (2-10).
- (b) At the applicable assembly step of the shock strut dressings:


- 1 Install the new lower cap 55-3573319-00 on the nuts (2-20).

- 2 Install the cotter pins (2-10) with their heads parallel to the bolt (2-40) head.

Make sure that the heads of the cotter pins are hard against castellations of the nuts (2-20). Bend the legs of the cotter pins laterally around the nut castellations to safety them: refer to PCS-7610.

NOTE: The cotter pins (2-10) are used to keep the lower cap

55-3573319-00 in position.

- 3 Apply sealant to the nuts (2-20) and the cotter pins (2-10): refer to

PCS-7200.

- 4 Install the new upper cap 55-3573317-00 on the lower cap 55-3573319-00.

- 5 Install the new cotter pins MS24665-155 through the upper cap

55-3573317-00 and the lower cap 55-3573319-00 and safety them: refer to PCS-7610.

- 6 Complete the assembly of the shock strut dressings: refer to ASSEMBLY in


the CMM.

### D. Re-Identification of equipment NOTE: When it is necessary to replace the identification nameplate identify the

replacement nameplate with all the applicable data.

(1) After you have done this Service Bulletin:

- - cancel the related strike number 10 on the 10-357303 LH Series and 10-357403 RH Series shock strut dressings nameplate, refer to M-DLPS1006-5 and cancel like this
- - write the Service Bulletin number 10-355-32-29 in the applicable aircraft and landing gear records.

#### E. Test

(1) Not applicable.

#### F. Close-up

(1) Refer to aircraft Service Bulletin A350-32-P031.

A. Refer to the Safran Landing Systems website (safrangroup.com) for the latest list of contact names, addresses and numbers.
